name: Salmon En Croute
recipeIngredient: Juice and Grated Rind of 1 Lemon
recipeInstructions:
1. Ask your fishmonger to fillet a 2-1/2 pound salmon and keep the bones and trimmings for the lemon cream sauce.
2. Preheat the oven to 200°C/400°F/Gas 6.
3. In a food processor, combine the white fish, breadcrumbs, lemon juice and rind, chives, parsley, egg, and oil until smooth. Season to taste.
4. Roll out the puff pastry to a long, oval shape that is at least 6 inches longer than the salmon fillets and twice as wide.
5. Place one salmon fillet in the center of the pastry. Spread over the white fish mixture and sandwich with the other salmon fillet.
6. Make diagonal cuts along each side of the puff pastry about 1/2 inch apart, leaving 3 inches at each end uncut.
7. Fold the sides together, criss-cross the cut strips over each other, and secure them with beaten egg.
8. Fold one end into a triangle to form the shape of a fish head and cut a wedge from the other end for the tail.
9. Place the fish on a greased baking tray. Brush the pastry with beaten egg.
10. Bake for 35-40 minutes. The fish is ready when a skewer inserted between the plaits comes out clean.
11. Serve on its own or with Cucumber Sauce (barry) or Lemon Cream Sauce (barry).
